title: Lua Cheat Sheet
description: Essential Lua commands and patterns for software engineers.

commands.description: Display the Lua version installed on your system
commands.scenario: Use when you need to verify the installed Lua version.
Run a Lua script to perform tasks defined in the script file.

commands.command: local sum = 0; for i = 1, 10 do sum = sum + i end; print(sum)
commands.description: Calculate and print the sum of numbers from 1 to 10
commands.scenario: Use to quickly calculate a sum in a Lua script.

commands.description: Insert a value into a Lua table
commands.scenario: Add elements to a table when building a list dynamically.

commands.description: Remove a value from a Lua table at a specific position
commands.scenario: Remove elements from a table when managing a list.

commands.description: Define a simple function to greet a user
commands.scenario: Create reusable functions for common tasks in Lua scripts.

commands.description: Execute an OS command from Lua
commands.scenario: Run shell commands from within a Lua script.
Include external modules to extend Lua script functionality.

commands.command: for k, v in pairs(t) do print(k, v) end
commands.description: Iterate over key-value pairs in a table
commands.scenario: Use to loop through all elements in a table.

commands.command: if x > 10 then print('x is greater than 10') end
commands.description: Conditional statement to check if a variable is greater than 10
commands.scenario: Perform conditional logic based on variable values.

commands.description: Set a metatable for a table
commands.scenario: Use metatables to change table behavior.

commands.description: Print a message to the console
commands.scenario: Output text to the console for debugging or user interaction.
Initialize the random number generator to produce different sequences.
